RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 235-BB, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Renee Reuter, a Republican, represents part of Jefferson County (District 112) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2022.
In addition to serving in the legislature, Reuter is an intellectual property attorney with Armstrong Teasdale in St. Louis. She previously worked as the first in-house intellectual property attorney for the largest car rental company in the world.
Prior to her service in the legislature, Reuter served for 12 years on the Jefferson County Council. She was the first chairman of the 7-member council and served as either chair or vice chair for seven of her 12 years on the council.
Reuter is a graduate of Windsor High School. She graduated cum laude from Colorado College with a bachelor’s degree in History and Political Science. Reuter obtained her Juris Doctorate from St. Louis University School of Law.
Reuter is a member of the Missouri Bar Association. She attends Immanuel Lutheran Church in Barnhart.
Born in St. Louis, Reuter now lives in Imperial with Liberty, her Labrador Retriever. She has two grown children, one grandson, and one granddaughter.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Requires school districts to provide instruction in cursive writing
Allows public officials to use campaign funds for childcare costs incurred while campaigning or performing official duties
Creates a special surcharge in civil cases for construction of a new courthouse in the twenty-third judicial circuit
Modifies provisions relating to motor vehicle assessment valuations
Modifies provisions of law relating to custody of in vitro human embryos
Changes the filing location for school board candidates
Creates a special surcharge in civil cases for construction of a new courthouse in the 23rd judicial circuit
Creates provisions relating to insurance coverage of genetic screenings for cancer risk
Modifies provisions relating to pyramid sales schemes
Adds an associate circuit judge to the 23rd judicial circuit
Allows for a cause of action for alienation of affection
Requires the air conservation commission to terminate by January 1, 2027, the operation of any motor vehicle emissions inspection program established under the Air Quality Attainment Act
Modifies provisions relating to the age of marriage
Creates provisions relating to the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline
Creates new provisions relating to the sale of kratom products
Modifies provisions relating to memorial highway designation requirements and costs
Creates the "Missing and Murdered African American Women and Girls Task Force"
Establishes the Educational Technology Impact Advisory Council

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Missouri Angel Investment Incentive Act"
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
